user.title: OpenWrt Setup Wizard
user.description: Select the connection method for your OpenWrt router. [Learn more]({docs_url}).

discovery.title: Searching for OpenWrt
discovery.description: Scanning your network for OpenWrt routers... {discovery_info}

select_device.title: Select Your Router
select_device.description:
Multiple OpenWrt devices were found. Please select the one you want to configure:

{public_info}

manual_entry.title: Manual Configuration
manual_entry.description: No routers were automatically detected, or you chose to enter details manually. Please provide the IP address and connection method.

credentials.title: Authentication

data.skip_random_mac: Ignore devices with randomized MAC addresses

ssh.title: SSH Authentication
ssh.description:
Enter the SSH credentials for {host}.

Note: If the port is left empty, it will default to 22. SSH is **not recommended** for full sensor support (like CPU usage on some models). Use LuCI RPC or ubus if possible.

[Security Best Practices]({security_link})

data.ssh_key: Private SSH Key (Optional, leaves password blank if used)
data.skip_random_mac: Ignore devices with randomized MAC addresses

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate
reauth_confirm.description: The credentials for the OpenWrt router are no longer valid. Please enter the new password.

confirm_discovery.title: Discovered OpenWrt Router
confirm_discovery.description:
Do you want to set up {name} ({host})? Connection will be established via **{method}**.

Available capabilities: {capabilities}

data.skip_random_mac: Ignore devices with randomized MAC addresses

permissions.title: Permissions Summary

data.acknowledge: I have reviewed this information and want to proceed

permissions_ubus.title: Permissions Summary
permissions_ubus.description:
Based on the credentials provided for user **{username}**, the following permissions were detected:

{permissions_table}

**Note:** UBUS permissions on some routers (especially newer versions or non-root users) can be very restrictive. They often block 'DHCP Leases' or 'System Temperature' even if the user has basic access.

If features are missing or sensors show 0, it usually means your user lacks ACL permissions for specific raw ubus objects. Try switching to **LuCI RPC** in the integration settings, which is often more compatible with restricted users.

You can proceed if this is expected.

data.acknowledge: I have reviewed this information and want to proceed

packages.title: Packages Summary & Feature Selection

data.acknowledge: I have reviewed this information and want to proceed

provision_user.title: Secure User Provisioning

data.mode: How would you like to proceed?

reuse_user.title: Existing User Credentials
reuse_user.description: Please enter the password for the existing **homeassistant** user on your router.

display_new_user.title: User Created Successfully

provision_failed.title: Provisioning Failed
provision_failed.description:
The automatic user provisioning failed.

**What to do now?**
- **Check Permissions:** Ensure your current user has `file.exec` rights.
- **Check Space:** Ensure your router has enough free space in `/usr/share/rpcd/`.
- **Try SSH:** SSH is often more robust for initial setup if ubus is restricted.

You can still proceed with the current credentials by skipping this step.

diagnostics.title: Connection Diagnostics

data.run_repair: Attempt automatic repair via SSH (start services / install missing packages)

repair_credentials.title: SSH Credentials for Repair
repair_credentials.description: To perform automatic repair, the integration needs root access to your router via SSH. Please provide the root credentials for SSH (usually username 'root'):

data.password: Password (Optional if using SSH key or blank)

repair_failed.title: Automatic Repair Failed
repair_failed.description:
The integration attempted automatic repair but failed:

```
{error}
```

Please check your router configuration or try configuring manually.

repair_success.title: Automatic Repair Successful
repair_success.description:
The integration successfully connected via SSH, started the required services and/or installed the missing packages.

The connection test is now passing. Click submit to continue setting up the integration.

ubus_restricted.title: Restricted Ubus Detected
ubus_restricted.description:
The connection to **{host}** ({model}) via **ubus** was successful, but the router is not returning information about wireless interfaces or system services.

To identify available features, the integration performed these checks:
1. `network.wireless status` -> Access Denied or No data
2. `rc list` (Services) -> Access Denied or No data

**Potential Causes:**
- **Manufacturer Lock:** Firmwares (like Xiaomi AX3600) often restrict the Ubus-HTTP interface.
- **ACL Restrictions:** Your user lacks permissions for these specific objects in `/etc/config/rpcd`.
- **Restricted User Feature:** Using LuCI's 'limited user' often only grants access to `luci` object RPCs, but not the raw Ubus objects needed here.

**Recommendation:** We strongly recommend going back and selecting **LuCI RPC** or **SSH** as the connection method. Otherwise, you will only see basic system sensors and no wireless clients or service controls.

data.acknowledge: I have reviewed this information and want to proceed

selective_tracking.title: Selective Device Tracking
selective_tracking.description:
To avoid overwhelming your Home Assistant with hundreds of device tracker entities, you can select only specific devices to track here. If you leave this empty, ALL connected devices will be tracked by default.

You can also enter manual MAC addresses (e.g. for devices currently offline).

mqtt_presence.title: MQTT Presence Detection (Optional)
mqtt_presence.description:
Enable MQTT-based presence detection for higher reliability and lower latency. This uses a third-party script from [f45tb00t/OpenWRT_HA_Presence]({presence_repo_url}).

⚠️ **Note:** This is a third-party feature. Support is not provided by this integration. Bugs should be reported to the original repository.

deploy_mqtt_presence.title: Deploying Scripts
deploy_mqtt_presence.description: Downloading and deploying the presence detection scripts to your router. This may take a moment...

deploy_failed.title: Deployment Failed
deploy_failed.description:
The deployment of the MQTT presence scripts failed.

**Error:**
```
{error}
```

Please check your router's internet connection and free space.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the router. Check the IP/Host and ensure the selected service (ubus/LuCI/SSH) is running.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid authentication credentials (Username, Password or SSH Key). Ensure the service (ubus/LuCI) is enabled and your user has sufficient ACL permissions.
error.timeout: Connection timed out. Check if the router is reachable and the port is correct.
error.ssl_error: SSL verification failed. If using self-signed certs, disable 'Verify SSL Certificate'.
error.package_missing: Required OpenWrt package is missing. For ubus: 'uhttpd-mod-ubus'. For LuCI: 'luci-mod-rpc'. Install via SSH: 'opkg update && opkg install PACKAGE' (or 'apk add PACKAGE' on newer versions).
error.permission_error: Insufficient RPC permissions. Check your user ACLs in /etc/config/rpcd. Note: 'Restricted users' in LuCI often only have access to web-interface RPCs, but not the raw ubus objects required for this connection method.
error.provision_failed: Failed to automatically create the dedicated user. Ensure you are connected as root and have enough free space on the router for 'rpcd' ACL files.
error.no_devices_found: No OpenWrt routers were automatically discovered on your network. You can try to enter the details manually.
error.mqtt_permission_missing: The configured OpenWrt user lacks the necessary permissions to deploy the MQTT presence detection scripts (requires file.exec/sys.exec rights).

abort.already_in_progress: Configuration flow is already in progress for this router.
abort.no_host: Discovered device has no valid host address.
abort.not_openwrt: Discovered device does not appear to be an OpenWrt router.

data.skip_random_mac: Ignore devices with randomized MAC addresses

options_redeploy_user.title: Redeploy Home Assistant User
options_redeploy_user.description:
To redeploy the Home Assistant user and its ACL rules, please provide the credentials for a user with root access (usually 'root').

This will recreate the dedicated user and restore all necessary permissions for the integration to work correctly.

options_redeploy_user_failed.title: Redeployment Failed
options_redeploy_user_failed.description:
The redeployment of the Home Assistant user failed.

**Error:**
```
{error}
```

Please check the credentials and ensure the router is reachable.

options_select_devices.title: Select Devices to Track
options_select_devices.description: Select the devices you want to track in Home Assistant. If none are selected, all discovered devices will be tracked.

data.manual_tracked_devices: Manual MAC Address Whitelist (One per line)

state.diagnostics_intro: The following checks were performed to identify the issue:
state.diagnostics_footer_intro: If you need help, copy this report and share it on the

auth_failed.title: Authentication failed for {host}
auth_failed.description:
The OpenWrt integration could not authenticate with {host}. The credentials might have been changed.

Please update the credentials to restore the connection.

wan_down.title: WAN connection is down on {host}
wan_down.description: The OpenWrt router {host} reports that the main WAN interface is currently down. Internet connectivity might be affected.

connection_lost.title: Connection lost to {host}
connection_lost.description: Home Assistant cannot reach the OpenWrt router at {host}. Please check if the device is powered on and reachable over the network.

missing_packages.title: Missing recommended packages on {host}
missing_packages.description:
To use all features of the OpenWrt integration, some packages are missing on {host}.

Please install the following packages via LuCI or SSH: `{packages}`.

firmware_outdated.title: OpenWrt firmware is outdated on {host}
firmware_outdated.description:
The OpenWrt router {host} is running version {current_version}, but version {latest_version} is available.

It is highly recommended to keep your router's firmware up to date for security reasons. You can download the latest release [here]({release_url}).

init.title: Refresh Permissions
init.description:
The permissions for user **{username}** on **{host}** need to be updated. You will be asked to provide the **root** password to perform this one-time maintenance task.

Note: The root password is only used for this session and is not stored.

root_login.title: Login as Root
root_login.description: Please enter the credentials for a user with **root** access on **{host}**.

abort.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the router as root.
abort.provision_failed: Failed to update permissions for the dedicated user.

stale_permissions_upgrade.title: Permissions lost after OpenWrt upgrade on {host}

init.title: Restore Permissions
init.description:
An OpenWrt firmware upgrade was detected on **{host}**. These upgrades often reset custom RPC ACL rules, which prevents the dedicated user **{username}** from accessing system sensors like CPU, RAM, or Temperature.

You need to redeploy the ACLs to restore full monitoring. You will be asked to provide the **root** password for this one-time fix.

Note: The root password is only used for this session and is not stored.

root_login.title: Login as Root
root_login.description: Please enter the credentials for a user with **root** access on **{host}**.

abort.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the router as root.
abort.provision_failed: Failed to update permissions for the dedicated user.

execute_command.name: Execute Command
execute_command.description: Executes a shell command on the OpenWrt router.

manage_service.name: Manage Service
manage_service.description: Manage an init.d service on the OpenWrt router.

service_name.name: Service Name
service_name.description: The name of the service (e.g., dnsmasq).

action.name: Action
action.description: The action to perform (start, stop, restart, enable, disable).

wake_on_lan.name: Wake on LAN
wake_on_lan.description: Send a Wake on LAN magic packet to a device via the OpenWrt router.

target.name: Target Router
target.description: The OpenWrt router to send the packet from.

mac.name: MAC Address
mac.description: The MAC address of the device to wake up.

interface.name: Interface
interface.description: Optional interface to send the packet through (e.g. br-lan).

uci_get.name: UCI Get
uci_get.description: Gets a UCI configuration value from the OpenWrt router.

uci_set.name: UCI Set
uci_set.description: Sets a UCI configuration value on the OpenWrt router and commits the changes.

create_backup.name: Create Backup
create_backup.description: Generates a backup archive of the router configuration.

generate_report.name: Generate Report
generate_report.description: Generates a detailed system report in markdown format.
